attributes.description: Alerts when average per-operation I/O latency exceeds 100 ms. Storage pressure degrades overall database performance.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Database I/O latency high

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server database I/O latency high

### What fired
`INCREASE(sqlserver.database.latency) / INCREASE(sqlserver.database.operations) > 0.1 s` during the window.

### Why it matters
High average I/O latency per operation means the storage backend is the bottleneck. Every query reading or writing waits longer.

### Triage
1. Split by file_type (data, log, filestream) and direction to localise.
2. Check OS wait types for PAGEIOLATCH* or WRITELOG.
3. Inspect storage layer metrics.

### Remediation
- Migrate files to faster storage.
- Reduce I/O-heavy workloads.

### Tuning
- 100 ms threshold; tighten for OLTP workloads.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Per-database file I/O latency (counter); INCREASE over window indicates storage pressure
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.database.latency IS NOT NULL
    AND metrics.sqlserver.database.operations IS NOT NULL
| STATS lat_increase = SUM(INCREASE(metrics.sqlserver.database.latency)),
       ops_increase = SUM(INCREASE(metrics.sqlserver.database.operations))
  BY instance_id = COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown"),
     database_name = COALESCE(resource.attributes.sqlserver.database.name, ""),
     file_type = COALESCE(attributes.file_type, file_type, ""),
     direction = COALESCE(attributes.direction, direction, "")
// Avg latency per op > 0.1s (100ms) when we have operations in the window
| WHERE ops_increase > 0 AND (lat_increase / ops_increase) > 0.1
| KEEP instance_id, database_name, file_type, direction, lat_increase, ops_increase
